electionFacts: India has the world's largest democratic electorate with over 968 million registered voters.
The Election Commission of India (ECI) was established on January 25, 1950.
India uses Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) since 1982 to ensure tamper-proof voting.
The NOTA (None of the Above) option was introduced in 2013.
India's general elections span multiple phases to manage logistics across the vast country.
Voters get an indelible ink mark on their finger to prevent double voting.
VVPAT machines were introduced to provide a paper trail for votes cast.
The minimum age to vote in India is 18 years since 1989 (lowered from 21).
